hook.text: The ocean has absorbed over 90% of the extra heat trapped by greenhouse gases since 1970.

buildup.text: Warmer water holds less oxygen, stressing fish and shifting species toward the poles.

discovery.text: Tropical fish are appearing in temperate waters, disrupting local food webs.

twist.text: Marine heatwaves—underwater heat spikes—have doubled in frequency since the 1980s.

climax.text: Warmer oceans fuel stronger hurricanes, bleach corals, and melt polar ice faster.

punchline.text: The ocean absorbed our heat. Now it's pushing back.

quiz.question: How much excess heat have oceans absorbed since 1970?
